title: HTML Entity Encoder & Decoder
description: Why do characters break your code? Discover the invisible symbols hiding in plain sight and the single click that makes them completely safe.

schemas.name: HTML Entity Encoder & Decoder
schemas.description: Why do characters break your code? Discover the invisible symbols hiding in plain sight and the single click that makes them completely safe.

mainEntity.name: What is an html entity encoder?

acceptedAnswer.text: An html entity encoder is a tool that converts special characters (like <, >, &, or quotes) into HTML entities. This ensures that the characters are safely displayed as text rather than being parsed as code by the web browser.

mainEntity.name: How do I encode html online?

acceptedAnswer.text: To encode html online, simply paste your text or HTML snippet into an online encoder tool. It instantly transforms reserved symbols into their corresponding HTML entities (like < or &), making it secure for web use.

HTML special characters are reserved symbols that have specific meanings in HTML syntax. The most common ones include the less-than sign (<), greater-than sign (>), ampersand (&), and quotation marks (\" and '). They must be converted to entities to prevent parsing errors.

mainEntity.name: Where can I find an html entities list?

acceptedAnswer.text: An html entities list provides a comprehensive mapping of characters to their named, decimal, and hexadecimal entity references. Many HTML entity encoders include a built-in reference table highlighting common symbols like math, currency, and arrows.

mainEntity.name: Why do I need to decode HTML entities?

acceptedAnswer.text: You need to decode HTML entities when you have raw source code or text that contains entities (like &quot;) and you want to convert them back into their readable character forms (like \") for editing, analysis, or displaying outside of a browser.

schemas.headline: HTML Entity Encoder & Decoder: Convert Special Characters Safely
schemas.description: Encode text to HTML entities or decode entities to text. Complete searchable reference table of all HTML entities.
